summary: Matched two effect-library functions, efLib_8005C2BC and efLib_8005F774, and updated several eflib.h prototypes with argument names. The functional work was small but decomp-relevant: it removed temporary variables/PAD_STACK from efLib_8005C2BC and rewrote efLib_8005F774's texture-object traversal into a compiler-matching form while preserving the color/TEV writes. The PR was described as part 2 of 3 for effect library functions and was verified by successful build and clang-format.
what_changed: Matched efLib_8005C2BC at 100% by simplifying object access: removed a local HSD_GObj* temporary and PAD_STACK, used GET_JOBJ(eff_1->gobj) directly, and passed eff_1->gobj directly to HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 on failure.
Matched efLib_8005F774 at 100% by restructuring traversal of an HSD_TObj linked list selected from HSD_JObjGetDObj(arg_jobj) and HSD_MObjGetTObj(dobj_1->mobj or NULL).

In efLib_8005F774, replaced generic decompiler temporaries such as temp_r0, var_ctr, var_ctr_2, and var_r4 with s32 ctr and s32 i, using an 8-step unrolled loop for arg1 >> 3 and a remainder loop for arg1 & 7.
Kept efLib_8005F774's final writes to tobj_1->tev->konst RGB from arg2 and tobj_1->tev->tev0 RGB from arg3, while removing commented-out alternative assignments.
Updated header prototypes to include argument names for efLib_8005C1B4, efLib_8005C2BC, efLib_8005CB34, efLib_8005E2B4, and efLib_8005F774.
smart_moves: Used direct field access eff_1->gobj in efLib_8005C2BC instead of carrying a temporary gobj_1, which also eliminated the need for stack padding in the matched version.

For efLib_8005F774, modeled the compiler-friendly linked-list skip as an unrolled eight-next loop plus a remainder loop, matching the original control flow more cleanly than the previous goto/block_7-style decompiler output.

Preserved the unsigned shift behavior for the quotient calculation with ctr = (u32) i >> 3 while still using signed local variables for the loop counters, indicating attention to codegen-sensitive typing.
Introduced a done label only for the zero-remainder fast path after the eight-step traversal, reducing awkward decompiler control flow while still matching.
Improved header readability by adding semantic placeholder argument names such as arg_gobj, arg_jobj, arg_vec3, and effect without claiming stronger names than the evidence supports.
decomp_lessons: Small local-variable changes can remove artificial PAD_STACK requirements when the compiler can reuse direct struct member access instead of preserving a named temporary.
When matching linked-list index traversal, splitting an index into quotient/remainder by eight and manually unrolling the quotient loop can be necessary to reproduce original PowerPC codegen.
Be careful with signedness in shifts: efLib_8005F774 uses a cast to u32 before shifting arg1 by 3, which is likely codegen-relevant.

Header updates with argument names are useful when they align with matched definitions and improve future call-site reasoning, but generic argN names are appropriate where semantics are not yet established.
Removing stale explanatory/commented assignments can clarify the final matched logic once the correct field writes are known.
naming_conventions: Effect-library functions retain address-based names in the efLib_8005XXXX style.
Header prototypes use lightweight argument names when semantics are limited: arg0, arg1, arg2, arg3, arg_gobj, arg_jobj, arg_vec3.
The PR used effect for an Effect* parameter in efLib_8005E2B4, showing a preference for simple semantic names when the pointee role is obvious.
Temporary decompiler names like temp_r0, var_ctr, var_ctr_2, var_r4, mobj_1, and gobj_1 were removed or replaced with simpler locals where possible.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed PAD_STACK(0x4) from efLib_8005C2BC after simplifying locals and direct gobj access.
Used GET_JOBJ(eff_1->gobj) and HSD_GObjPLink_80390228(eff_1->gobj) directly to avoid an extra HSD_GObj* local.
In efLib_8005F774, obtained the texture object via HSD_JObjGetDObj followed by HSD_MObjGetTObj, explicitly calling HSD_MObjGetTObj(NULL) when no dobj exists rather than guarding the call away.

Matched traversal by computing ctr = (u32) i >> 3, advancing tobj_1 through eight explicit tobj_1 = tobj_1->next statements per loop, then applying i &= 7 and advancing the remaining count in a do/while loop.
Used a done label to bypass the remainder traversal when i & 7 is zero after the unrolled blocks.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states tests passed: build succeeds and clang-format passes.
follow_up_queries: Find part 1 and part 3 of effect library function matching to compare naming and traversal patterns.
Search for other efLib_8005Fxxx functions that traverse HSD_TObj linked lists or write tev->konst/tev0 color channels.
Check whether efLib_8005C1B4 and efLib_8005C2BC share a common matching pattern around GET_JOBJ and HSD_GObjPLink_80390228.
Look for future PRs that replace arg_gobj/arg_jobj/arg_vec3 with more semantic names once effect-library behavior is better understood.
